issues.claim: Analog does not equally apply elsewhere
issues.objection: Force-fit test for 7.30: the analog (thread 3 (knowing-at-departure-of-mind; STRETCHED; chapter closes)) is anchored to the verse's specific structural claim; it would not apply to a randomly chosen alternate verse. The verse's central image — for 7.30, the image specifically tied to thread 3 (knowing-at-departure-of-mind; STRETCHED; chapter closes) — is what the engineering scenario depends on; substituting another verse's image breaks the analog. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Chapter-closing verse. Heavy metaphysics: adhibhūta/adhidaiva/adhiyajña + prayāṇa-kāla. Forward-pointer to Ch 8 honored explicitly. Engineering analog scoped at career-end-departure substrate-articulation. Counter-example: performative all-hands talk that does not survive the team's actual problems is the failure mode.

notes: Force-fit test for 7.30: the engineering analog cannot equally well derive from a different verse. Tags (long-term-thinking, first-principles, operator-system-coupling) are anchored on this verse's specific claim. PASS.
